Making VSCode's Tailwind CSS IntelliSense Work With React JSX
Tailwind CSS IntelliSense worked fine in CRA (Create React App), but for some reason after I moved to Vite for React, it stopped working. This post is just a note on how I fixed it.
In a CRA (Create React App) setup, writing Tailwind CSS gives me the IntelliSense dropdown like usual:
But for some reason, after switching to Vite for React, it doesn't work:
At first I suspected the file extension, but Vite uses
by default, so that wasn't it. Tailwind CSS IntelliSense reads
, so I also wondered if my config was wrong:
language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
But that was fine too. Eventually I found this thread:
Tailwind CSS IntelliSense does not provide suggestions in a ReactJS project?
The post is actually about CRA config, but it made me think of Emmet-related settings. Try this:
language-json shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
And just like that, Tailwind CSS IntelliSense works in Vite for React.
The weird thing is that CRA (Create React App) does get IntelliSense working out of the box, but Vite doesn't — I'm guessing the Tailwind CSS IntelliSense extension added CRA support at some point.
